summary: Preinstall hook exfiltrates host identifiers to hardcoded C2 and DNS beacon
details: @klapp-otp/routes@99.0.1 declares a preinstall hook (`node index.js`) that fires automatically on `npm install`. index.js collects host identifiers — `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `__dirname`, `process.cwd()`, and the package name — and ships them to two attacker-controlled destinations: an HTTP POST to the bare IP `http://172.201.213.59:9090/c` carrying the JSON payload, and an out-of-band DNS lookup to a hex-encoded subdomain of `oast.live` (an interactsh-style exfil channel). The package contains no functional code beyond this beacon; the description is a stub ('security research'), and the inflated 99.0.1 version is consistent with a dependency-confusion lure targeting an internal `@klapp-otp/*` scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
